Based on this information, the mental  health professional can decide if your child would benefit  from an intervention and what intervention might work best.  A comprehensive evaluation of a child’s mental health  usually involves:  ● A parent interview to discuss the child’s developmental  history, temperament, relationships with friends and  family, medical history, interests, abilities, and any  prior treatment ● Information gathering from the child’s school, such as  standardized test scores and reports on behavior,  c
